projectSummary.uploadedFileName: JY255038 - Geotechnical Engineering Report - Chase Bank - signed.pdf
projectSummary.location: Crossroads Dr. SW and U.S. Hwy 14, Rochester, MN
projectSummary.primaryConcern: Undocumented existing fill (4 to 8.7 feet thick) with debris and obstruction in B-3 area

waterTable.evidenceSnippet: Water observed at 17 feet; 17.4 feet while drilling.
Water observed at 16.9 feet; 17.2 feet while drilling.
Water observed at 17.3 feet; 17.4 feet while drilling.

soilTypes: Fill (clayey sand / silty sand) to ~6 ft
Native poorly graded sand (SP / SP-SM) to 20 ft
borings.notes: Fill over native sand. Groundwater at ~17 ft.

soilTypes: Native poorly graded sand (SP) and silty sand (SM)

borings.notes: Auger refusal at 8.7 ft. Offset borings B-3A and B-3B also refused at same depth.

soilTypes: Native poorly graded sand with silt

foundation.summary: Maximum net allowable bearing pressure of 3,000 psf on recompacted native sand or engineered fill.
Minimum embedment: 60 inches (exterior unheated), 42 inches (heated), 12 inches (interior heated).
Estimated total settlement less than about 1 inch.

slab.summary: Minimum 6 inches of free-draining crushed aggregate beneath slabs.
Estimated modulus of subgrade reaction = 125 pci.

pavement.summary: Asphalt section: 4 inches ACC (two lifts) over 8 inches aggregate base.
Concrete section: 5 inches PCC over 6 inches aggregate base.
Use 7-inch PCC in dumpster and heavy-load areas.

riskFlags.evidence: Existing fill encountered 4 to 8.7 feet thick.
riskFlags.description: Undocumented fill unsuitable for direct foundation support; removal and replacement required under building.

riskFlags.evidence: Wood, glass, Styrofoam and concrete observed in B-3.
riskFlags.description: Construction debris and buried concrete may require excavation and disposal.

riskFlags.evidence: Groundwater observed at approximately 17 feet in three borings.
riskFlags.description: Seasonal groundwater fluctuations may affect deep utilities and subgrade stability.

chatResponses.response: Groundwater was encountered in three borings (B-1, B-2, B-4) at depths of approximately 17 feet. Other borings terminated above the water table.
Undocumented fill ranging from 4 to 8.7 feet thick was encountered across the site. Debris and buried concrete were found in the B-3 area.
Primary risks are undocumented fill, buried debris, and localized obstructions near B-3. The site is suitable for shallow foundations after proper removal and recompaction.
